summary: Iteration 7 (Scale 6 pays — multi-country mass duplication) ALL PASSING. Backend: 9/9 test_iteration7.py (scale 2 countries with fresh 50 steps + unique primary_language per country + daily_budget=30/clone + scaled_from + batch_id; invalid country → 400; empty target_countries → 422 pydantic; custom_domains map correctly per country; invalid domain → 400; duplicate domain across countries → 400; scale-siblings from source and from clone both work; copy_products=true clones as status=draft with cloned_from set; cross-operator → 403) PLUS 4/4 test_iteration7_extra.py that I added to cover gaps (cross-site domain uniqueness → 409 from ANY other site; generate_ads_copy=true → ads_copy_scheduled=N; total_daily_budget_eur scales linearly 3×30=90; copy_products=false → empty catalog even when source has products). Regression: 8/8 iter6 + 15/15 iter5 still pass (23/23). Frontend E2E as Concepteur: login OK; /sites lists 10 sites; opened Luméa Confort detail; 'Scaler 6 pays' button visible and clickable; ScaleModal opens with 6 country cards (FR DE CH BE UK NL) PRE-SELECTED by default, each showing flag + language (FR/DE/FR/FR/EN/NL) + currency (EUR/EUR/CHF/EUR/GBP/EUR) + 30€/jour; toggling countries updates the 'PAYS CIBLES (N SÉLECTIONNÉS)' counter and the 'BUDGET PUB TOTAL' live (4 × 30 = 120€/jour correctly displayed); domain inputs visible only for selected countries; copy_products + generate_ads toggles work; Lancer sur 4 pays button submits, ScaleResult view appears showing '4 clones créés ! Budget total : 120€/jour' with 4 tiles each testid=scale-result-{CH,BE,UK,NL}, correct flag, 'Ouvrir →' link (href=/sites/{id}); Terminer (scale-result-close) testid present.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/scale.py clone status is hardcoded 'active' regardless of source.status (same flag as iter6 on duplicate.py) — if source was paused/archived the clones still go live. Minor product decision.

routes/scale.py: no transactional rollback — if insert_many(products) or domain write fails after sites insert, the clones exist with partial data. For a batch of 5 clones this could leave the portfolio in an inconsistent state; wrap per-clone work in try/except with cleanup on failure.

routes/scale.py domain DB-wide uniqueness check excludes site_id (source) but does NOT check collisions between the N newly-inserted clones in the same batch vs EACH OTHER for DOMAINS already written earlier in the loop. Low risk because used_domains set guards against duplicates in the incoming payload, but if two concurrent scale requests race they could both pass the pre-check and then both insert the same domain (no unique index at insert-time). Consider adding a unique partial index on custom_domain (already recommended in iter6 review).

routes/scale.py _generate_ads_background swallows ALL exceptions including budget 402 — correct per spec, but logs via exception() which will flood logs in prod if budget is exhausted for a 5-country scale. Consider downgrading to logger.warning for the known 402 case and only logger.exception() for unexpected errors.

routes/scale.py: operator scaling their own site sets new_operator = user['id'] — good. But admin scaling on behalf sets new_operator = source.operator_id — what if source has no operator_id (e.g., admin-created site with no operator)? Then new_operator = None and the clones are orphaned. Add explicit fallback to user['id'].

routes/scale.py scale_batch_id is generated AFTER sites are inserted then patched with update_many. If the process crashes between insert and update_many, clones exist without batch_id. Minor — generate batch_id upfront and include it in the initial insert payload instead.

routes/scale.py ScaleInput.tone has no enum/regex validation — if an unknown tone value is passed it propagates to Claude prompts. Add Literal['rassurant','direct','premium',...] based on what ads_copy expects.

SiteDetail.jsx ScaleModal: all 6 countries are pre-selected by default which could surprise users clicking 'Lancer sur 6 pays' and accidentally creating 6 sites × 30€ = 180€/jour budget commitment. Consider defaulting to 0 selected + requiring explicit opt-in, OR adding a confirmation toast showing the total budget before firing.
test_credentials: concepteur@conceptfactory.fr / Concepteur2026! and admin@conceptfactory.fr / Factory2026! (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md) — both working.
seed_data_creation: Pytest created transient sites (TEST_ScaleSrc-*, TEST_Other-*, ScaleSource-*, AdminOnly-*) — each test cleans up its scaled children via admin fixture. UI E2E created 4 Luméa Confort clones (Suisse, Belgique, Royaume-Uni, Pays-Bas). All cleaned up post-test via admin DELETE (verified: 10 leftover sites deleted successfully, only the 9 operator-seeded sites + Luméa Confort remain).
context_for_next_testing_agent: test_iteration7.py (9 tests, ~3s) + test_iteration7_extra.py (4 tests, ~45s incl. ads_copy background wait) together give full scale endpoint coverage. ScaleModal testids: scale-site, scale-modal, scale-modal-close, scale-country-{FR|DE|CH|BE|UK|NL}, scale-domain-{CC}, scale-copy-products, scale-generate-ads, scale-tone, scale-confirm. ScaleResult testids: scale-result, scale-result-{CC}, scale-result-close. IMPORTANT: all 6 countries are pre-selected by default in the modal — clicking a country TOGGLES it. For a fresh source site without selected_countries, all 6 are offered; for sites that already have some countries set, only missing ones appear.
rca of the issue: No issues found. All 16 acceptance criteria from the review request verified end-to-end. The scale endpoint correctly derives language per country (FR→fr, DE→de, CH→fr, BE→fr, UK→en, NL→nl), sets daily_budget_eur=30 per clone, generates fresh 50-step workflow per clone, shares scale_batch_id across siblings, handles optional domain mapping with proper uniqueness checks (400 invalid shape, 400 dup within payload, 409 cross-site), supports optional product cloning (draft status + cloned_from ref), schedules ads_copy as BackgroundTasks (fire-and-forget), and enforces operator ownership (403). Frontend modal matches backend contract 1:1 with correct live budget summary.
